    2/4 What is software engineering?

    1 minute read

    Intro

    • HCI is kind of psy branch

    • This(software engineering) is a bit more business branch

    • software is very different

    • key point to remember: Programming is actually a small part of software engineering

    • S.E. is literally the field of how to engineer software

    • EX) building a building
      • knowing how to hammer a nail and how to lay a brick is not enough
      • What else is needed?

    High Level S.E.

    At a high level, SE는 steps of the development process를 생각함

    • Requirements(how do we know what to build?)
    • Design / Specification
    • Programming / Construction / Implementation
    • Testing (Verification and Validation)
    • Production and Release
    • Maintenance and Updates(bugs etc)

    Why is S.E. different?

    What makes creating software different than creating other things that engineers build (ex. machines, aircraft, bridges etc)?

    • Software is changeable/malleable (소프트웨어는 가변성이다)
    • Requirements often change DURING development
    • Requirements often change AFTER development
    • Misconceptions regarding actual time frames / how software is built and maintained from customers… and developers

    Problems about failure

    • how do we protect software from being failed?
    • how do we make sure the software is safe?
      • ex) HealthCare.gov failed launch

    Find some failures

    • Find a major issues making software crashed
    • What was the technical cause?
    • What was the underlying cause?
      • A problem with requirements?
      • No enough testing?
      • Training?
    • How would you mitigate this in the future?

    Categories: cs3240

    Updated: February 3, 2021
